coachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime: first spike lands around minute 12 (Wraith Leggings), third item by minute 24. Take Vesh: +11.1% on your burst combo at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

offMeta.honestAbsence: no defensible off-meta option this patch (no underexplored item clears the 8% objective edge vs the popular build)

metaBuilds.title: Magical Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 24 — about 2,776 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +18% more 10s rotation damage: try Wraith Leggings over Azure Core (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 4,330 effective HP vs physical in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Brawler
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for extended fights vs bruisers, online around minute 24 — about 2,783 damage over a 20s fight in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +20% more extended fights vs bruisers: try Wraith Leggings over Azure Core (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. The Fey deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Timewarp: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 11% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Renna's main threat is Mortal Coil; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Timewarp: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 11% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Argus deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Timewarp: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 11% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Countess deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Timewarp: their 3s all-in loses 28% of its bite; you give up 11% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Iggy & Scorch deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Timewarp: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 11% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Lt. Belica executes low targets with Neural Disruptor — don't linger below half HP.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Timewarp: their 3s all-in loses 31% of its bite; you give up 11% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Infinite Impact” ⇒ poke; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field midlane 49.2% over 285,733 games, magnitude not simulated.
